role: Co-founder of the Ahmadzadeh-Pouyan group and theorist of the Organization of Iranian People's Fadai Guerrillas
bio: Massoud Ahmadzadeh (1947–1972) was an Iranian Marxist guerrilla theorist and co-founder of the armed underground cell that became known as the Ahmadzadeh-Pouyan group. Together with [Amir Parviz Pouyan](/people/amir-parviz-pouyan), he developed an influential framework for armed struggle against the Pahlavi monarchy, articulated in his pamphlet *Armed Struggle: Both a Strategy and a Tactic* (1970). The Ahmadzadeh-Pouyan group merged with [Bijan Jazani](/people/bijan-jazani)'s group in 1971 to form the [Organization of Iranian People's Fadai Guerrillas](/orgs/organization-of-iranian-peoples-fadai-guerrillas), a founding act symbolised by the [Siahkal incident](/events/siahkal-incident-1971). Ahmadzadeh was arrested by [SAVAK](/orgs/savak) and executed in May 1972. He is remembered as one of the founding martyrs of the Fadaiyan movement.
editorial_notes: Co-founder of the Ahmadzadeh-Pouyan group. Executed 1972.
